Calorie Deficit Calculator

Calculate your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) and the calorie deficit needed to reach your weight loss goal safely.

FAQ

What is a calorie deficit?

A calorie deficit occurs when you consume fewer calories than your body burns. A deficit of 500 calories/day leads to approximately 1 pound of fat loss per week.

How much calorie deficit is safe?

A deficit of 500-1000 calories per day (1-2 lbs/week loss) is generally considered safe. Never go below 1200 calories/day for women or 1500 for men.

What is TDEE?

TDEE (Total Daily Energy Expenditure) is the total number of calories you burn per day including exercise and daily activities.
